items.hook: Pathe, Tricky Knot, Saatchi and Logical Pictures execs to speak at Screen’s 2026 UK finance Summit
items.body: The ‘Financing the Future of UK Film’ summit takes place on September 29 at the BFI Southbank in London.

items.hook: Hidden Creatures by Dino Martins review – the revolting world of parasites
items.body:
From maggots to viruses, this gross-out compendium also manages to celebrate the awe and inventiveness of nature

items.hook: The Tolkien Ensemble is a Danish ensemble which created "the world's first complete musical interpretation of the poems and songs from The Lord of the Rings".
items.body: They published four CDs from 1997 to 2005, in which all the poems and songs of The Lord of the Rings are set to music. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Macao is the 31st novel in the long-running Nick Carter-Killmaster series of spy novels.
items.body: Carter is a US secret agent, code-named N-3, with the rank of Killmaster. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Precioso Palma was a novelist and playwright in the Philippines.
items.body: He wrote the novel Ipaghiganti Mo Ako…!. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The population was 2,119 at the 2020 census.
items.body: Hinsdale is a town in Cattaraugus County, New York, United States. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Anniversary by Andrea Bajani review – meet the terrible parents
items.body:

items.hook: Juan de la Cuesta was a Spanish printer known for printing the first editions of Don Quixote de la Mancha (1605) and the Novelas ejemplares (1613), by Miguel de Cervantes, as well as the works of other leading figures of Spain's Golden Age, such as Lope de Vega.
